scope_periods.description: Latest fiscal years ended December 31, 2022 (FY22), 2023 (FY23) and the latest trailing twelve months period (e.g. TTM Aug-24)

phases.description: Sell-side Financial Due Diligence and Data & Analytics — full FDD report including QoE, revenue/profitability analysis, balance sheet, working capital, and comprehensive D&A workstream
phases.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

phases.description: Post-bid support — data room advisory, monthly/quarterly report updates, buyer diligence calls, feedback on buyer inquiries
phases.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

phases.description: Pre-bid support to December [20], 2024 — limited to sell-side FDD materials review, traffic light assessment of QoE adjustments

Post-bid full buy-side FDD: Overview, Consistency of accounting policies, QoE, Revenue/gross margin, Expenses, FY24 Outturn, Working capital, AR, Prepaids, AP/accrued liabilities, Net debt, CapEx, Related parties, Commitments & contingencies, Optional procedures

sections.heading: Overview
appears_in: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Obtain background information of Target, including its:
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Organizational structure, ownership and management reporting relationships;
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Range of customer segments and regions served; and
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Financial reporting and information systems (including process, timing, structure of financial reporting, and interaction with the information/accounting systems).

sections.heading: Consistency of accounting policies
appears_in: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Read Target's financial statements and discuss them with Target's management to gain an understanding of accounting policies and practices including:
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Reporting methodology;
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Intercompany transactions and cost allocations;
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Revenue recognition policies and billing practices;
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Significant accounting estimates; and
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles.
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

sections.heading: Sell-side Quality of Earnings assessment
appears_in: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json
sections.note: Phase 1 (pre-bid) scope — limited review of sell-side QoE materials

bullets.text: Based on the analysis and discussion with the sell-side due diligence advisor, comment on Management's proposed adjustments to reported EBITDA (i.e. Executive compensation changes, personal expenses, one-time / non-recurring items, out-of-period adjustments, truck recovery rate increases, other etc.) and inquire about the nature and rationale of such adjustments and supporting documentation;
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Perform a traffic light assessment of Management and sell-side proposed adjustments;
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Propose any significant adjustments identified that are not reflected in the sell-side Quality of Earnings analysis; and
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Comment on any potential findings that could have material impacts to enterprise / equity value.
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

sections.heading: Quality of Earnings (Consolidated & Service Line and Monthly basis)

bullets.text: Obtain details on the Company's historical financial performance and propose potential adjustments to historical earnings before interest taxes, depreciation, amortization ("EBITDA") for the periods noted earlier by considering:
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Understand the impact of and validate adjustments identified by management and its financial sponsor ownership, including adjustments made for bank covenant purposes
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Identify other abnormal, non-recurring and extraordinary one-time adjustments
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Related party transactions – Enquire into the existence and appropriateness of related party transactions and balances and their impact on reported results going forward, including those with shareholders, management and affiliated companies
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Consider movements in significant balance sheet accounts with a focus upon the nature, composition and basis of valuation of these accounts particularly around judgmental areas that would impact EBITDA (e.g. reserves and other accruals, inventory costing, accounts receivable, etc.).
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Analyze the impact of COVID-19 on the Company's EBITDA from both revenue (e.g.: increase / decrease in demand) and cost perspectives (e.g.: personal protective equipment costs, office costs, etc.)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Analyze the one-time impact of elevated inflation, tight labor market and elevated absenteeism
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Understand impact of cost allocations (as listed in the Target's internal management reporting package)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Impact of cash vs. GAAP costs for the following:
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: pension expense (non-cash services costs - if applicable)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Bonuses (actually paid out vs expensed)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Bad debt (expense vs actual write-offs)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Warranty expense (actual claims)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Non-cash compensation (for Profits Interest Units)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Normalization of vendor purchaser rebates (to the extent required)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Analyze the pro forma impact of:
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Pre-acquisition results (on an adjusted basis) on acquired businesses (AB May in 2022)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Updated Pricing Agreements with Customers
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Quantifiable synergies that management has identified and believe are applicable (i.e. on prior acquisitions)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Retained costs sitting within Corporate
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Understand impact of Quality of Earnings adjustments on the FY23 budget
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

sections.heading: Data & Analytics
appears_in: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json
KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Flacon_Aug 8.json
sections.note: Comprehensive D&A workstream with four sub-areas; deliverables include PowerPoint trend report, Power BI dashboard, and consolidated Excel sales cube

bullets.text: The purpose of this section is to perform detailed analysis on the drivers of revenue and margin growth, marketing performance, and operations to understand upside opportunities and communicate an effective story in conjunction with the Quality of Earnings. The outputs will be a PowerPoint trend report, highlighting key insights; a Power BI dashboard that Imperial / Cowen can use to 'slice & dice' and extract visuals, this can also be shared with select buyers; and a consolidated Excel sales cube. Steps below are dependent on data from the brands
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: The purpose of this section is to perform detailed analysis on the drivers of revenue and margin growth, marketing performance, and operations to understand upside opportunities and communicate an effective story in conjunction with the Quality of Earnings. The outputs will be a PowerPoint trend report, highlighting key insights; a Power BI dashboard that Imperial / Cowen can use to 'slice & dice' and extract visuals, this can also be shared with select buyers; and a consolidated Excel sales cube. Steps below are dependent on data from the brands
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Flacon_Aug 8.json

sections.heading: Revenue and Profitability Analysis (D&A)
appears_in: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json
KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Flacon_Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Understands the health and sustainability of revenue and margin; covers last fiscal years (2020 – 2022) and TTM 2023
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Leveraging KPMG's proprietary SPI (strategic profitability insights) tool, use the transaction / sales database from each entity (Haley, A.B. May, and Korte), analyze revenue and profitability (cost, price, margin), key KPIs (e.g., number of jobs, average sales price, etc.) and performance trends (e.g., concentration, new-lost-existing customers, price-volume-mix, high growth services, OEM/equipment used, etc.); evaluating by product category/service offering, (e.g., Heating, Cooling, Plumbing, Electrical, etc.), sub-category/service type (e.g., AC installs, AC repairs, AC maintenance, etc.) and by channel (e.g., Google, Social Media, Direct Campaign, referral, etc.), by service type, by geography (zip code, county, etc.), by branch, by customer, by end market, etc.
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Perform a service-level line item analysis to understand profitability (including price, volume, and mix calculations; low margin service areas; basket analysis, etc.), opportunities to optimize pricing, areas to rationalize, reflect cost increases, drive cross-sell / improve baskets, etc.
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Extract and identify top brands / OEMs used in servicing customers and understand any opportunities to harmonize pricing across different business that might result in upside
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Where available, link above sales / job data to technician utilization and salary to understand performance opportunities
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Consolidate the data into a clean data cube that can be leveraged by potential buyers
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Extract and understand any patterns and trends that might inform value creation and optimization opportunities for the next buyer
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Understands the health and sustainability of revenue and margin; covers last fiscal years (2020 – 2022) and TTM 2023
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Flacon_Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Analyze health of the customer base across the three entities, in terms of revenue / profitability by customer, type of customer (residential, commercial, etc.) any customer contracts, etc.; overall retention, growth patterns, renewal history, cohort (vintage); new, loss, existing; customer concentration; etc. including associated services and categories these customers purchase to understand patterns and insights
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Analyze type of revenue and mix (one-time versus recurring), across different streams (e.g., revenues from HVAC & Electrical, from Repair Services, from Extended Warranty, and Construction), and understand any performance trending insights
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Extract and understand any patterns and trends that might inform value creation and optimization opportunities for the next buyer

bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Analyzes trends and insights related to revenue, customers, average revenue/margin per job, recurring nature of customers, high value products, etc. by lead source /channel by brand and advertising spend by lead source / channel by brand

bullets.text: Extracts insights into drivers of profitability and opportunities for value creation / optimization by the next buyer

bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Analyzes revenue generating job (e.g., service visit, install, replacement, etc.) and ties back to performance
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Evaluates the performance of marketing and advertising; data is analyzed in conjunction with the revenue and customer analysis to understand any trends / insights in relation to how marketing and advertising drives performance; covers last fiscal years (2020 – 2022) and TTM 2023
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Flacon_Aug 8.json

bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Flacon_Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Extracts insights into drivers of profitability and opportunities for value creation / optimization by the next buyer

sections.heading: Operations Performance (D&A)
appears_in: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json
KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Flacon_Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Evaluates the performance of operations; where appropriate, ties back this analysis to the previous steps; covers last fiscal years (2020 – 2022) and TTM 2023
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Analyze employee census by role, position, annual salary, and bonus; where appropriate (e.g., technicians, sales people) tie back to revenue / margin performance to understand returns (e.g., salary vs sales or utilization multiples) and other associated metrics (e.g., revenue per tech, utilization, margin by tech, etc.)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Evaluate technician turnover metrics by brand and whether it is voluntary or involuntary; further evaluate against tenure (<90 days vs. >90 days); where appropriate tie back to sales and compensation
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Analyze the fleet of each brand respectively, including number of vehicles, age of vehicles, lease vs. owned, maintenance cost per vehicle, average cost of replacement, etc.; where appropriate, tie back to technician performance (e.g., revenue / margin)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Analyze suppliers, including total purchases, type of material, typical markups to customers ,etc.; understand rebates vs. volume discounts; where possible, tie back to job type (e.g., HVAC, electrical) and overall job performance (e.g., revenue / margin)
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text: Assess financing partners, including the number of jobs that are finance, type, average selling price, financing partners, terms, etc.; where appropriate, tie back to salesperson, lead channel, etc. to understand opportunities to optimize
bullets.source: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json

bullets.text:
Calls with R&W insurers / lenders
Conduct call with potential R&W insurers / lenders and answer any Q&A as it pertains to our engagement.

bullets.text: Obtain and read year-end review working papers for the last fiscal year and perform the following:
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.text: Discuss any significant issues with the external accountants;
bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

bullets.source: Project Celsius - KPMG SOW - DRAFT - 09-Dec-24.json

workstreams_found.note: Primary FDD workstream in all 3 files — full scope extracted

sources: KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Aug 8.json
KPMG Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work - Flacon_Aug 8.json
workstreams_found.note: Comprehensive D&A workstream covering revenue/profitability, customer base health, marketing/advertising, and operations performance. Deliverables include SPI tool analysis, PowerPoint trend report, Power BI dashboard, and Excel sales cube.

notes: The Aug 8 and Flacon_Aug 8 files are near-identical — same Frontier Service Partners target, same scope structure. Likely variants addressed to different parties. Both are sell-side VDD SOWs with Phase 1 (full FDD + D&A) and Phase 2 (post-bid support).

Project Celsius is a buy-side FDD with Phase 1 (pre-bid: limited sell-side QoE assessment) and Phase 2 (post-bid: comprehensive buy-side FDD). This reflects the HVAC industry pattern of Phase 1 pre-bid / Phase 2 post-bid.

HVAC SOWs have a strong emphasis on service-line-level analysis (by brand/entity), technician-level KPIs (utilization, turnover, revenue per tech), fleet management, and marketing/lead-channel analytics.
The sell-side SOWs include explicit D&A workstream with SPI tool usage, Power BI dashboards, and data cube consolidation — this is more extensive than typical FDD-only SOWs.
Project Celsius references POC (percentage of completion) revenue recognition and commodity pass-through mechanisms, indicating the target has both service and project/construction segments.
Only FDD and D&A content was extracted. No Tax DD, IT DD, or HR DD workstreams were present in these files.
